locator.raw: Executive summary, p.xi, Table 2 (Haemoglobin cutoffs to define anaemia in individuals and populations)
locator.section: Executive summary, p.xi, Table 2 (Haemoglobin cutoffs to define anaemia in individuals and populations)

applicability.ageRange: Children 6 months-14 years, both sexes; age-banded (6-23 mo, 24-59 mo, 5-11 y, 12-14 y girls/boys); apparently-healthy reference basis; applies at individual and population level

laboratory.assayMethod: Cutoffs defined on haemoglobin concentration via a 5th-percentile statistical derivation; stated as not tied to a specific analyzer but assumes standardized haemoglobin measurement

locator.raw: Executive summary, p.xi, Normative statement 1.a (Haemoglobin cutoffs to define any anaemia)
locator.section: Executive summary, p.xi, Normative statement 1.a (Haemoglobin cutoffs to define any anaemia)

applicability.ageRange: Children 6-23 months, both sexes -- the only pediatric group whose defining cutoff was changed in the 2024 revision

laboratory.assayMethod: 5th-percentile statistical cutoff derivation; stated as not method-dependent

locator.raw: Section 1 (What should be the haemoglobin cutoffs...), scope/definitions narrative, para 'Physiological, environmental, and genetic factors...'
locator.section: Section 1 (What should be the haemoglobin cutoffs...), scope/definitions narrative, para 'Physiological, environmental, and genetic factors...'

applicability.ageRange: All age and lifecycle groups including children (explicitly 'early life ... and with age'); context = defining/interpreting haemoglobin cutoffs

laboratory.assayMethod: not applicable (qualitative interpretive principle)

locator.raw: Executive summary, p.xii-xiii, Normative statement 2.a.1 (Elevation of residence) + Table 4 and equation footnote b
locator.section: Executive summary, p.xii-xiii, Normative statement 2.a.1 (Elevation of residence) + Table 4 and equation footnote b

applicability.ageRange: All population groups including children; residents/workers at elevation, esp. below 2500 m (>98% of world population)

laboratory.assayMethod: Adjustment applied to the haemoglobin concentration value (subtract from observed Hb, or add to the cutoff); not analyzer-dependent

locator.raw: Executive summary, p.xiv, Normative statement 2.b (Infection and inflammation)
locator.section: Executive summary, p.xiv, Normative statement 2.b (Infection and inflammation)

applicability.ageRange: All groups including children in infection/inflammation- and malaria-endemic settings

laboratory.assayMethod: Haemoglobin is NOT inflammation-adjusted (contrast: serum ferritin / transferrin receptor commonly are); not analyzer-dependent

locator.raw: Introduction / methods narrative, para 'This guideline updates and complements previous recommendations...'
locator.section: Introduction / methods narrative, para 'This guideline updates and complements previous recommendations...'

applicability.ageRange: not applicable (provenance / methodology statement)

laboratory.assayMethod: not applicable

locator.raw: Page 5, 'Recommendations: Laboratory diagnosis' (first two bullets)
locator.section: Page 5, 'Recommendations: Laboratory diagnosis' (first two bullets)

applicability.ageRange: All patients suspected of G6PD deficiency (males and females; applies across ages including neonates/children); not age-band-restricted

laboratory.assayMethod: Quantitative spectrophotometric G6PD activity assay (WHO one-substrate or Glock & McLean method); screening/POC tests are insufficient to confirm or exclude, especially in females

locator.raw: Page 6, 'Factors affecting clinical interpretation' (opening paragraph)
locator.section: Page 6, 'Factors affecting clinical interpretation' (opening paragraph)

applicability.ageRange: Any patient tested during or soon after acute hemolysis/reticulocytosis (all ages); heightened for African-type variant

laboratory.assayMethod: Quantitative G6PD activity assay; interpretation is reticulocyte-dependent — result must be read alongside the reticulocyte count on the same sample

locator.raw: Page 6, 'Factors affecting clinical interpretation'; corroborated by Page 5, 'Recommendations: Laboratory diagnosis' (third bullet)
locator.section: Page 6, 'Factors affecting clinical interpretation'; corroborated by Page 5, 'Recommendations: Laboratory diagnosis' (third bullet)

applicability.ageRange: Patient with clinically suspected G6PD deficiency and unexplained hemolysis, initial assay normal/equivocal (all ages)

laboratory.assayMethod: Repeat quantitative G6PD activity assay after reticulocytosis resolves

locator.raw: Page 7, 'Molecular diagnosis' (opening paragraph)
locator.section: Page 7, 'Molecular diagnosis' (opening paragraph)

applicability.ageRange: Recently transfused patients, heterozygous females, or cases where reticulocytosis may mask deficiency (all ages)

laboratory.assayMethod: Enzyme activity assay may be unreliable post-transfusion (donor cells) or with reticulocytosis; molecular/DNA analysis is the confirmatory alternative

locator.raw: Page 3, Table I 'Classification of G6PD variants' (Class / Residual G6PD activity (% of normal))
locator.section: Page 3, Table I 'Classification of G6PD variants' (Class / Residual G6PD activity (% of normal))

applicability.ageRange: Hemizygous males (footnote: '*Levels of residual G6PD activity in hemizygous males'); classification framework applies across ages

laboratory.assayMethod: Residual activity as % of the laboratory's normal (quantitative assay); Class I defined clinically (CNSHA), not purely biochemically

locator.raw: Page 6, 'Normal values' (adult reference values paragraph)
locator.section: Page 6, 'Normal values' (adult reference values paragraph)

applicability.ageRange: Adults (explicitly 'For adults'); NOT applicable to neonates/infants, whose values deviate (see ev_007). Each lab should establish its own reference range (ICSH or commercial kit).

laboratory.assayMethod: Quantitative spectrophotometric assay; values are temperature-dependent (30 C vs 37 C) and 6-PGD-correction-dependent; expressed per gram haemoglobin (iu = umol substrate converted per minute)

locator.raw: Page 6, 'Normal values' (newborn/premature paragraph)
locator.section: Page 6, 'Normal values' (newborn/premature paragraph)

applicability.ageRange: Newborns and infants; premature infants born between 29 and 32 weeks gestation vs term neonates

laboratory.assayMethod: Quantitative G6PD activity assay; adult reference ranges do not apply to neonates/infants (age- and gestational-age-dependent)

locator.raw: Section 'Initial screening blood lead level' / heading 'If the patient's BLL is ≥ 3.5 micrograms per deciliter'
locator.section: Section 'Initial screening blood lead level' / heading 'If the patient's BLL is ≥ 3.5 micrograms per deciliter'

applicability.ageRange: Children (childhood lead poisoning prevention); both sexes; CDC screening context cites Medicaid-enrolled testing at ages 12 and 24 months, or 24-72 months if never screened

laboratory.assayMethod: Blood lead level measured in µg/dL; capillary (finger-prick/heel-prick) or venous sample for initial screening; capillary result at/above BLRV requires a venous sample

locator.raw: Section 'Initial screening blood lead level', first paragraph
locator.section: Section 'Initial screening blood lead level', first paragraph

applicability.ageRange: Children undergoing initial blood lead screening; both sexes

laboratory.assayMethod: Method-dependent: capillary (finger/heel-prick) initial screen vs venous confirmatory draw; venous draw recommended for confirmatory BLL screening

locator.raw: Table 1 — 'Recommended schedule for obtaining a confirmatory venous sample'
locator.section: Table 1 — 'Recommended schedule for obtaining a confirmatory venous sample'

applicability.ageRange: Children with an elevated initial screening capillary BLL; both sexes

laboratory.assayMethod: Timelines keyed to initial screening capillary BLL; confirmation is by venous sample

locator.raw: Section 'If the patient's BLL is 3.5-19 micrograms per deciliter', iron-deficiency bullet
locator.section: Section 'If the patient's BLL is 3.5-19 micrograms per deciliter', iron-deficiency bullet

applicability.ageRange: Children with confirmed BLL 3.5-19 µg/dL; both sexes

laboratory.assayMethod: Iron-deficiency testing/treatment thresholds deferred to AAP guidelines; not specified numerically in this CDC document

locator.raw: Headings 'If the patient's BLL is 20-44 micrograms per deciliter' and 'If the patient's BLL is ≥ 45 micrograms per deciliter'
locator.section: Headings 'If the patient's BLL is 20-44 micrograms per deciliter' and 'If the patient's BLL is ≥ 45 micrograms per deciliter'

applicability.ageRange: Children with confirmed BLL 20-44 µg/dL or ≥45 µg/dL; both sexes

laboratory.assayMethod: Tiers keyed to confirmed venous BLL; not analyzer-specific in this document

locator.raw: Section 'Classification of anemia' (refers to Figure 2)
locator.section: Section 'Classification of anemia' (refers to Figure 2)

applicability.ageRange: Infants and children with anemia (individual-patient diagnostic workup); not applicable to a single numeric cutoff

laboratory.assayMethod: Automated hematology analyzer (MCV) plus reticulocyte count; classification is method-dependent on analyzer-reported indices

locator.raw: Section 'Normocytic anemia' (first sentence)
locator.section: Section 'Normocytic anemia' (first sentence)

applicability.ageRange: Pediatric patients (age band not sub-specified in this sentence); note the review's own Figure 1/Table 1 show MCV reference limits are age-dependent in children, so this 80-100 fL band is a general anchor, not an age-specific cutoff

laboratory.assayMethod: Automated hematology analyzer-reported MCV; method-dependent

locator.raw: Section 'Laboratory investigation'
locator.section: Section 'Laboratory investigation'

applicability.ageRange: Infants and children with anemia; interpretive rule, not applicable to a single sex/age band

laboratory.assayMethod: Reticulocyte count (automated or manual); interpretation is relative to degree of anemia, no fixed numeric cutoff given

locator.raw: Section 'Laboratory investigation' (peripheral blood smear)
locator.section: Section 'Laboratory investigation' (peripheral blood smear)

applicability.ageRange: Infants and children with anemia; qualitative smear findings, not applicable to a numeric cutoff

laboratory.assayMethod: Peripheral blood smear microscopy (morphology); not a numeric threshold

locator.raw: Section 'BM replacement syndromes'
locator.section: Section 'BM replacement syndromes'

applicability.ageRange: Infants and children with anemia plus another cytopenia; qualitative pattern, not applicable to a numeric cutoff

laboratory.assayMethod: CBC differential plus peripheral blood smear; not a numeric threshold

locator.raw: Section II (Background), four-criteria enumeration (pp. 5-6)
locator.section: Section II (Background), four-criteria enumeration (pp. 5-6)

applicability.ageRange: not applicable

laboratory.assayMethod: not applicable

locator.raw: Section II (Background), Criterion (4) verbatim (p. 6); also Section IV(4), pp. 14-15
locator.section: Section II (Background), Criterion (4) verbatim (p. 6); also Section IV(4), pp. 14-15

applicability.ageRange: not applicable

laboratory.assayMethod: not applicable

locator.raw: Section IV(3) (Interpretation of Criterion 3), pp. 10-11
locator.section: Section IV(3) (Interpretation of Criterion 3), pp. 10-11

applicability.ageRange: not applicable

laboratory.assayMethod: not applicable

locator.raw: Section IV(4)(a) (Interpretation of Criterion 4), p. 14
locator.section: Section IV(4)(a) (Interpretation of Criterion 4), p. 14

applicability.ageRange: not applicable

laboratory.assayMethod: not applicable

locator.raw: Section IV(4) (Interpretation of Criterion 4), p. 15
locator.section: Section IV(4) (Interpretation of Criterion 4), p. 15

applicability.ageRange: not applicable

laboratory.assayMethod: not applicable

locator.raw: Screening > Laboratory Screening (paragraph beginning 'Screening to identify children and adolescents with ID and IDA includes')
locator.section: Screening > Laboratory Screening (paragraph beginning 'Screening to identify children and adolescents with ID and IDA includes')

applicability.ageRange: Infants, children, and adolescents (birth through adolescence) being screened for ID/IDA; general primary-care and subspecialty context

laboratory.assayMethod: CBC on an automated cell counter; serum ferritin immunoassay. Not a single numeric threshold — test-selection statement

locator.raw: Table 1 — 'Normal Hematologic Parameters in Infants, Children, and Adolescents by Age and Sex, From Age 9 Months to 18 Years' (ref 42), Hemoglobin / MCV / RDW rows; columns 6–24 mo, 2–6 y, 6–12 y, 12–<18 y (Females, Males)
locator.section: Table 1 — 'Normal Hematologic Parameters in Infants, Children, and Adolescents by Age and Sex, From Age 9 Months to 18 Years' (ref 42), Hemoglobin / MCV / RDW rows; columns 6–24 mo, 2–6 y, 6–12 y, 12–<18 y (Females, Males)

applicability.ageRange: Females and males, 6–24 mo / 2–6 y / 6–12 y / 12–<18 y (table title states 'From Age 9 Months to 18 Years'); age- and sex-stratified

laboratory.assayMethod: Automated CBC analyzer; intervals derived from reference source cited as ref 42. Local laboratory intervals should be used where available

locator.raw: Diagnosis > Iron Studies (final sentence of paragraph reviewing SF thresholds, following WHO and ASH threshold discussion)
locator.section: Diagnosis > Iron Studies (final sentence of paragraph reviewing SF thresholds, following WHO and ASH threshold discussion)

applicability.ageRange: Young and school-aged children (≤20 ng/mL) vs adolescents and all menstruating individuals (≤30 ng/mL); age- and menstruating-status stratified

laboratory.assayMethod: Serum ferritin (SF) immunoassay; interpretation confounded by inflammation (see ev_004). AAP threshold differs from WHO <12/<15 ng/mL and aligns with ASH ≤20/≤30 ng/mL

locator.raw: Diagnosis > Iron Studies (paragraph beginning 'In most otherwise healthy children without evidence of acute infection or chronic disease')
locator.section: Diagnosis > Iron Studies (paragraph beginning 'In most otherwise healthy children without evidence of acute infection or chronic disease')

applicability.ageRange: Children/adolescents with acute or chronic inflammation, tissue injury, or acute illness; contrasted with clinically well children

laboratory.assayMethod: Serum ferritin interpreted alongside C-reactive protein (CRP); qualitative interpretive rule, no numeric CRP cutoff stated

locator.raw: Diagnosis > Iron Studies (paragraph on soluble transferrin receptor); corroborated by Table 4 (sTfR1) row
locator.section: Diagnosis > Iron Studies (paragraph on soluble transferrin receptor); corroborated by Table 4 (sTfR1) row

applicability.ageRange: Children with chronic inflammatory conditions and risk factors for ID; used to separate ID from anemia of chronic inflammation

laboratory.assayMethod: Soluble transferrin receptor (sTfR/sTfR1) assay combined with serum ferritin; index = sTfR/log10(ferritin). Less affected by inflammation than SF; limited availability and lack of published pediatric norms

locator.raw: Laboratory Diagnosis of IDA > Complete Blood Cell Count (paragraph beginning 'The most valuable laboratory test'); Table 3 'Differential Diagnosis of Children With Microcytic Anemia'
locator.section: Laboratory Diagnosis of IDA > Complete Blood Cell Count (paragraph beginning 'The most valuable laboratory test'); Table 3 'Differential Diagnosis of Children With Microcytic Anemia'

applicability.ageRange: Children/adolescents with microcytic anemia; differential across IDA, alpha/beta thalassemia trait, and anemia of inflammation/chronic disease

laboratory.assayMethod: CBC (MCV, RDW) plus SF/iron panel; beta thalassemia trait via elevated Hb A2 on Hb analysis after 6 months of age (ID falsely normalizes Hb A2); qualitative pattern, not a single cutoff

locator.raw: Treatment of Severe or Refractory IDA > Severe IDA (opening sentences)
locator.section: Treatment of Severe or Refractory IDA > Severe IDA (opening sentences)

applicability.ageRange: Young children and adolescents with confirmed IDA; category applies within an established IDA diagnosis, not to anemia of any cause

laboratory.assayMethod: Hemoglobin from CBC; category is diagnostic-severity classification, not an executable transfusion rule (transfusion tied to hemodynamic instability / blood loss, not the number alone)
